milestoneTitle: Phase M9.13 — Fixed vignette overlay replaces per-tile activation fade. A new html::after fixed-position gradient (pointer-events:none, z-index:5, above content but below the z:100 navbar) darkens the top and bottom quarters of every viewport with warm-greige tones and stays fully transparent through the center ~50%. Panels sitting under that overlay visually dim; panels in the middle band read crisp. Because the effect is a spatial layer instead of a per-panel opacity / bg animation, it doesn't compete with the activation transition for budget, can be tuned in isolation, and satisfies the new 'prefer static / spatial solutions' rule recorded in CLAUDE.md (called out by the user after prior regressions stacking animations on glass). M9.12's per-tile bg flip on activation is removed — tiles now hold one baseline value and the overlay does the work. Activation itself is lighter-touch: only a single box-shadow lift on .dash-panel.is-activated / .learn-zone.is-activated. Observer band widens from the prior narrow 20% (-40% rootMargin) to 50% (-25% rootMargin) and extends to .learn-zone so Learn panels lift identically as they pass through. New CLAUDE.md bullet + memory entry added.
